title: Coherent (Resonance) Breathing
blurb: Slow, even breaths at about six per minute.
lead: Coherent or resonance breathing means slowing to about five to six breaths per minute, which balances the nervous system and supports calm.

blocks.text: Coherent breathing means breathing slowly and evenly — roughly five to six breaths per minute, with equal inhales and exhales.

This pace is close to the body's 'resonance frequency', where heart rate and breathing sync in a way that supports calm and emotional balance. It has reasonable research support for reducing stress and anxiety.

items: Breathe in gently through the nose for about 5 seconds.
Breathe out gently for about 5 seconds.
Keep the breath smooth and unforced — no holding.
Continue for 5–20 minutes; a guide or app helps keep the pace.

items.label: Evidence
items.value: Supported by research on resonance/HRV breathing

blocks.text: Breathing too deeply. Aim for slow and smooth, not big — comfort keeps it sustainable.

resources.title: Healthy Minds Program
resources.whyHere: Free training that includes slow, balanced breathing.
